item.title: Bayesian Parameter Estimation of 3-Weibull Distribution
item.description:
The problem of parameter estimation for a Weibull distribution is of significant interest in survival analysis. However, many point estimators like MLE, momemts method,and MAP estimates using iterative EM or BFGS methods fail especially when the shape parameter is small and/or the left truncation is high. I (in team of two) worked on studying and implementing Bayesian inference of the unknown parameters of the Weibull distribution.

We followed an approach of factorizing the posterior density of shape and scale parameter into a marginal distribution (of scale parameter) conditioned on data, and a conditional posterior density (of shape parameter) conditioned on the scale parameter and data. We assumed that the posterior distribution for shape parameter is a log-concave function and approximated it to a gamma distribution with unknown hyperparameters. This helped in constructing a Gibbs sampling procedure and obtaining the Bayes estimate of our parameters along with credible intervals.
